$schema_doc: Each entry tracks one memory framework the auto-bench routine has considered. Status values: 'integrated' (adapter merged), 'candidate' (adapter being developed), 'rejected' (does not meet decision rules), 'failed' (adapter exists but smoke test failed), 'needs-revisit' (re-evaluate when upstream changes). Tier values: 'local-with-cloud-llm' (memory backend local, LLM API cloud — ACCEPTED), 'local-with-local-llm' (everything local incl. inference — REWRITE config to the fairness-baseline provider), 'saas-only' (memory operations require vendor SaaS — REJECTED). Fairness-baseline provider: Sophnet (https://www.sophnet.com/api/open-apis/v1) serving openai/gpt-4.1-mini.

$decision_rules.accept: Memory store / search operations run in a local process or local container. LLM and embedding calls may go to the fairness-baseline provider (Sophnet) or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint reachable from the cloud runner.
$decision_rules.rewrite: If the system defaults to local LLM / embedding inference, force-rewrite its config to use the fairness baseline via LLM_BASE_URL + LLM_API_KEY (Sophnet, model openai/gpt-4.1-mini) before benchmarking.
$decision_rules.reject: Any system whose memory operations require calling a vendor-hosted endpoint (e.g. *.mem0.ai, api.getzep.com cloud-only mode) is rejected, even if a self-hosted version is theoretically possible but not packaged.

systems.name: evermemos
systems.notes: Native system. Fork target. Routine should never modify this adapter.

systems.name: mem0
systems.rejection_reason: EverOS upstream evaluates Mem0 via MemoryClient (cloud), which violates 'local memory backend' rule. The mem0 Python package does expose a local Memory() class — reconsider as 'needs-revisit' if a local-mode adapter is contributed.
systems.notes: If you decide to add a local-mode adapter, change status to 'candidate' and remove from routine deny-list.

systems.name: memos
systems.rejection_reason: Upstream evaluation uses MEMOS_KEY against vendor API. Same revisit logic as Mem0.

systems.name: memu
systems.rejection_reason: Upstream evaluation uses MEMU_API_KEY against vendor API.

systems.name: zep
systems.rejection_reason: Community Edition deprecated; only Graphiti engine remains open source. No clean self-hosted memory path that matches upstream evaluation conditions.

systems.name: simplemem
systems.rejection_reason: ephemeral install failed: repository v0.2.0 root is not a Python project (no top-level pyproject.toml or setup.py); SDK is exposed only via 'from main import SimpleMemSystem' after a manual clone. PyPI publishes simplemem==0.1.0 (Jan 2026), which lacks the v0.2.0 add_dialogue/add_text/finalize/query API used by this adapter.

last_run.last_error: uv run --with 'simplemem @ git+https://github.com/aiming-lab/SimpleMem.git@v0.2.0': Failed to download and build — does not appear to be a Python project, as neither pyproject.toml nor setup.py are present in the directory.
last_run.notes: Adapter file is left in place targeting the documented v0.2.0 API surface (add_text/finalize/query with tag-based user isolation). To make this benchmarkable, upstream needs to package the repo (add a top-level pyproject.toml exposing 'simplemem' as a real Python package) OR we need to switch the adapter to a clone+sys.path strategy and import 'from main import SimpleMemSystem' directly. Marking [install-failed] per auto-bench rules.
